info.title: Find registry and scaffold touchpoints (@explore subagent)

parts.text: Single task: locate exact places/tests to update for registering a new tool and scaffold comment. Return file paths and exact relevant snippets/line references for registry.go, registry_test.go, and internal/agent/agent.go. Thoroughness quick.

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
10: )
11: 
12: // ExecContext holds the minimal context needed by generic tool executors.
13: type ExecContext struct {
14: 	Workdir string
15: 	Stderr  io.Writer
16: 	Verbose bool
17: }
18: 
19: // ToolEntry holds a tool's definition and executor functions.
20: type ToolEntry struct {
21: 	Definition func() provider.Tool
22: 	Execute    func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult
23: }
24: 
25: // Registry maps tool names to their definitions and executors.
26: // Registration must happen at startup before any concurrent Dispatch calls.
27: // Concurrent Dispatch calls (read-only) are safe after registration is complete.
28: type Registry struct {
29: 	entries map[string]ToolEntry
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRegistry returns a new Registry with an empty, initialized entries map.
33: func NewRegistry() *Registry {
34: 	return &Registry{
35: 		entries: make(map[string]ToolEntry),
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // Register adds a tool entry to the registry, keyed by name.
40: // If a tool with the same name already exists, it is silently replaced.
41: func (r *Registry) Register(name string, entry ToolEntry) {
42: 	r.entries[name] = entry
43: }
44: 
45: // Has returns true if a tool with the given name exists in the registry.
46: func (r *Registry) Has(name string) bool {
47: 	_, ok := r.entries[name]
48: 	return ok
49: }
50: 
51: // Resolve takes a list of tool names and returns their provider.Tool definitions.
52: // Each entry's Definition() is called on every Resolve invocation (not cached).
53: // Returns an error if any name is not found in the registry or has a nil Definition.
54: // Returns an empty non-nil slice for nil or empty input.
55: func (r *Registry) Resolve(names []string) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
56: 	if len(names) == 0 {
57: 		return []provider.Tool{}, nil
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	tools := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(names))
61: 	for _, name := range names {
62: 		entry, ok := r.entries[name]
63: 		if !ok {
64: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", name)
65: 		}
66: 		if entry.Definition == nil {
67: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no definition", name)
68: 		}
69: 		tools = append(tools, entry.Definition())
70: 	}
71: 	return tools, nil
72: }
73: 
74: // Dispatch looks up the tool by call.Name and executes it.
75: // Returns (ToolResult, nil) on success, or (zero ToolResult, error) for
76: // unknown tools or tools with nil executors.
77: func (r *Registry) Dispatch(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
78: 	entry, ok := r.entries[call.Name]
79: 	if !ok {
80: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", call.Name)
81: 	}
82: 	if entry.Execute == nil {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no executor", call.Name)
84: 	}
85: 	return entry.Execute(ctx, call, ec), nil
86: }
87: 
88: // RegisterAll registers all implemented tool entries into the given registry.
89: // Both cmd/run.go and ExecuteCallAgent call this after NewRegistry().
90: // Future milestones add lines here — no call-site changes needed.
91: // Safe to call multiple times (Register silently replaces duplicates).
92: func RegisterAll(r *Registry) {
93: 	r.Register(toolname.ListDirectory, listDirectoryEntry())
94: 	r.Register(toolname.ReadFile, readFileEntry())
95: 	r.Register(toolname.WriteFile, writeFileEntry())
96: 	r.Register(toolname.EditFile, editFileEntry())
97: 	r.Register(toolname.RunCommand, runCommandEntry())
98: 	r.Register(toolname.URLFetch, urlFetchEntry())
99: }

(End of file - total 99 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bytes"
5: 	"context"
6: 	"strings"
7: 	"testing"
8: 
9: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
10: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
11: )
12: 
13: func TestNewRegistry(t *testing.T) {
14: 	r := NewRegistry()
15: 	if r == nil {
16: 		t.Fatal("NewRegistry() returned nil")
17: 	}
18: }
19: 
20: func TestRegistry_Register_And_Has(t *testing.T) {
21: 	r := NewRegistry()
22: 
23: 	// Has returns false before registration
24: 	if r.Has("test_tool") {
25: 		t.Fatal("Has returned true for unregistered tool")
26: 	}
27: 
28: 	// Register a tool
29: 	r.Register("test_tool", ToolEntry{
30: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
31: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "test_tool"}
32: 		},
33: 	})
34: 
35: 	// Has returns true after registration
36: 	if !r.Has("test_tool") {
37: 		t.Fatal("Has returned false for registered tool")
38: 	}
39: 
40: 	// Has returns false for a different name
41: 	if r.Has("other_tool") {
42: 		t.Fatal("Has returned true for unregistered tool")
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	// Register same name again — silent replacement
46: 	r.Register("test_tool", ToolEntry{
47: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
48: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "test_tool", Description: "replaced"}
49: 		},
50: 	})
51: 	tools, err := r.Resolve([]string{"test_tool"})
52: 	if err != nil {
53: 		t.Fatalf("Resolve after replacement returned error: %v", err)
54: 	}
55: 	if tools[0].Description != "replaced" {
56: 		t.Errorf("expected replaced description, got %q", tools[0].Description)
57: 	}
58: }
59: 
60: func TestRegistry_Resolve_KnownTools(t *testing.T) {
61: 	r := NewRegistry()
62: 	r.Register("tool_a", ToolEntry{
63: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
64: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "tool_a", Description: "Tool A"}
65: 		},
66: 	})
67: 	r.Register("tool_b", ToolEntry{
68: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
69: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "tool_b", Description: "Tool B"}
70: 		},
71: 	})
72: 
73: 	tools, err := r.Resolve([]string{"tool_a", "tool_b"})
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		t.Fatalf("Resolve returned unexpected error: %v", err)
76: 	}
77: 	if len(tools) != 2 {
78: 		t.Fatalf("expected 2 tools, got %d", len(tools))
79: 	}
80: 	if tools[0].Name != "tool_a" {
81: 		t.Errorf("expected tools[0].Name = %q, got %q", "tool_a", tools[0].Name)
82: 	}
83: 	if tools[1].Name != "tool_b" {
84: 		t.Errorf("expected tools[1].Name = %q, got %q", "tool_b", tools[1].Name)
85: 	}
86: }
87: 
88: func TestRegistry_Resolve_UnknownTool(t *testing.T) {
89: 	r := NewRegistry()
90: 	r.Register("tool_a", ToolEntry{
91: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
92: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "tool_a"}
93: 		},
94: 	})
95: 
96: 	_, err := r.Resolve([]string{"tool_a", "nonexistent"})
97: 	if err == nil {
98: 		t.Fatal("Resolve should return error for unknown tool")
99: 	}
100: 	if !strings.Contains(err.Error(), `unknown tool "nonexistent"`) {
101: 		t.Errorf("error should contain unknown tool message, got: %v", err)
102: 	}
103: }
104: 
105: func TestRegistry_Resolve_Empty(t *testing.T) {
106: 	r := NewRegistry()
107: 
108: 	// Empty slice
109: 	tools, err := r.Resolve([]string{})
110: 	if err != nil {
111: 		t.Fatalf("Resolve([]string{}) returned error: %v", err)
112: 	}
113: 	if tools == nil {
114: 		t.Fatal("Resolve([]string{}) returned nil slice, expected empty non-nil")
115: 	}
116: 	if len(tools) != 0 {
117: 		t.Fatalf("expected 0 tools, got %d", len(tools))
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	// Nil input
121: 	tools, err = r.Resolve(nil)
122: 	if err != nil {
123: 		t.Fatalf("Resolve(nil) returned error: %v", err)
124: 	}
125: 	if tools == nil {
126: 		t.Fatal("Resolve(nil) returned nil slice, expected empty non-nil")
127: 	}
128: 	if len(tools) != 0 {
129: 		t.Fatalf("expected 0 tools, got %d", len(tools))
130: 	}
131: }
132: 
133: func TestRegistry_Resolve_NilDefinition(t *testing.T) {
134: 	r := NewRegistry()
135: 	r.Register("bad_tool", ToolEntry{
136: 		Definition: nil,
137: 		Execute: func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult {

139: 		},
140: 	})
141:

143: 	if err == nil {
144: 		t.Fatal("Resolve should return error for nil definition")
145: 	}
146: 	if !strings.Contains(err.Error(), `has no definition`) {
147: 		t.Errorf("error should contain 'has no definition', got: %v", err)
148: 	}
149: }
150: 
151: func TestRegistry_Dispatch_KnownTool(t *testing.T) {
152: 	r := NewRegistry()
153: 	r.Register("echo_tool", ToolEntry{
154: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
155: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "echo_tool"}
156: 		},
157: 		Execute: func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult {
158: 			return provider.ToolResult{
159: 				CallID:  call.ID,
160: 				Content: "echoed: " + call.Arguments["input"],
161: 			}
162: 		},
163: 	})
164: 
165: 	result, err := r.Dispatch(context.Background(), provider.ToolCall{
166: 		ID:        "call-1",
167: 		Name:      "echo_tool",
168: 		Arguments: map[string]string{"input": "hello"},
169: 	}, ExecContext{})
170: 
171: 	if err != nil {
172: 		t.Fatalf("Dispatch returned unexpected error: %v", err)
173: 	}
174: 	if result.CallID != "call-1" {
175: 		t.Errorf("expected CallID %q, got %q", "call-1", result.CallID)
176: 	}
177: 	if result.Content != "echoed: hello" {
178: 		t.Errorf("expected Content %q, got %q", "echoed: hello", result.Content)
179: 	}
180: 	if result.IsError {
181: 		t.Error("expected IsError to be false")
182: 	}
183: }
184: 
185: func TestRegistry_Dispatch_UnknownTool(t *testing.T) {
186: 	r := NewRegistry()
187: 
188: 	_, err := r.Dispatch(context.Background(), provider.ToolCall{
189: 		ID:   "call-1",

201: func TestRegistry_Dispatch_NilExecutor(t *testing.T) {
202: 	r := NewRegistry()
203: 	r.Register("nil_tool", ToolEntry{
204: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
205: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "nil_tool"}
206: 		},

208: 	})
209: 
210: 	_, err := r.Dispatch(context.Background(), provider.ToolCall{
211: 		ID:   "call-1",

213: 	}, ExecContext{})
214: 
215: 	if err == nil {
216: 		t.Fatal("Dispatch should return error for nil executor")
217: 	}
218: 	if !strings.Contains(err.Error(), `has no executor`) {
219: 		t.Errorf("error should contain 'has no executor', got: %v", err)
220: 	}
221: }
222: 
223: func TestRegistry_Dispatch_PassesExecContext(t *testing.T) {
224: 	var capturedEC ExecContext
225: 
226: 	r := NewRegistry()
227: 	r.Register("capture_tool", ToolEntry{
228: 		Definition: func() provider.Tool {
229: 			return provider.Tool{Name: "capture_tool"}
230: 		},
231: 		Execute: func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult {
232: 			capturedEC = ec
233: 			return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: "ok"}
234: 		},
235: 	})
236: 
237: 	stderrBuf := &bytes.Buffer{}
238: 	ec := ExecContext{
239: 		Workdir: "/tmp/test-workdir",
240: 		Stderr:  stderrBuf,
241: 		Verbose: true,
242: 	}
243: 
244: 	_, err := r.Dispatch(context.Background(), provider.ToolCall{
245: 		ID:   "call-1",
246: 		Name: "capture_tool",
247: 	}, ec)
248: 
249: 	if err != nil {
250: 		t.Fatalf("Dispatch returned unexpected error: %v", err)
251: 	}
252: 	if capturedEC.Workdir != "/tmp/test-workdir" {
253: 		t.Errorf("expected Workdir %q, got %q", "/tmp/test-workdir", capturedEC.Workdir)
254: 	}
255: 	if capturedEC.Stderr != stderrBuf {
256: 		t.Error("expected Stderr to be the provided buffer")
257: 	}
258: 	if capturedEC.Verbose != true {
259: 		t.Error("expected Verbose to be true")
260: 	}
261: }

<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent/agent.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package agent
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"errors"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"path/filepath"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
11: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
13: )
14: 
15: // MemoryConfig holds memory sub-configuration for an agent.
16: type MemoryConfig struct {
17: 	Enabled    bool   `toml:"enabled"`
18: 	Path       string `toml:"path"`
19: 	LastN      int    `toml:"last_n"`
20: 	MaxEntries int    `toml:"max_entries"`
21: }
22: 
23: // ParamsConfig holds model parameter overrides for an agent.
24: type ParamsConfig struct {
25: 	Temperature float64 `toml:"temperature"`
26: 	MaxTokens   int     `toml:"max_tokens"`
27: }
28: 
29: // SubAgentsConfig holds sub-agent execution configuration for an agent.
30: type SubAgentsConfig struct {
31: 	MaxDepth int   `toml:"max_depth"`
32: 	Parallel *bool `toml:"parallel"`
33: 	Timeout  int   `toml:"timeout"`
34: }
35: 
36: // AgentConfig represents a parsed agent TOML configuration file.
37: type AgentConfig struct {
38: 	Name          string          `toml:"name"`
39: 	Description   string          `toml:"description"`
40: 	Model         string          `toml:"model"`
41: 	SystemPrompt  string          `toml:"system_prompt"`
42: 	Skill         string          `toml:"skill"`
43: 	Files         []string        `toml:"files"`
44: 	Workdir       string          `toml:"workdir"`
45: 	Tools         []string        `toml:"tools"`
46: 	SubAgents     []string        `toml:"sub_agents"`
47: 	SubAgentsConf SubAgentsConfig `toml:"sub_agents_config"`
48: 	Memory        MemoryConfig    `toml:"memory"`
49: 	Params        ParamsConfig    `toml:"params"`
50: }
51: 
52: // Validate checks that required fields are present in the agent configuration.
53: // It checks name first (fail-fast): if name is missing, it returns that error
54: // without checking model.
55: func Validate(cfg *AgentConfig) error {
56: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Name) == "" {
57: 		return errors.New("agent config missing required field: name")
58: 	}
59: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Model) == "" {
60: 		return errors.New("agent config missing required field: model")
61: 	}
62: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth < 0 {
63: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.max_depth must be non-negative")
64: 	}
65: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth > 5 {
66: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.max_depth cannot exceed 5")
67: 	}
68: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.Timeout < 0 {
69: 		return errors.New("sub_agents_config.timeout must be non-negative")
70: 	}
71: 	if cfg.Memory.LastN < 0 {
72: 		return errors.New("memory.last_n must be non-negative")
73: 	}
74: 	if cfg.Memory.MaxEntries < 0 {
75: 		return errors.New("memory.max_entries must be non-negative")
76: 	}
77: 	validTools := toolname.ValidNames()
78: 	for _, name := range cfg.Tools {
79: 		if !validTools[name] {
80: 			return fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q in tools config", name)
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 	return nil
84: }
85: 
86: // Load reads and parses an agent TOML configuration file by name.
87: // The name parameter is the agent name without the .toml extension.
88: func Load(name string) (*AgentConfig, error) {
89: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
90: 	if err != nil {
91: 		return nil, err
92: 	}
93: 
94: 	path := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents", name+".toml")
95: 
96: 	if _, err := os.Stat(path); os.IsNotExist(err) {
97: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent config not found: %s", name)
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
101: 	if err != nil {
102: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read agent config %q: %w", name, err)
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	var cfg AgentConfig
106: 	if _, err := toml.Decode(string(data), &cfg); err != nil {
107: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse agent config %q: %w", name, err)
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	if err := Validate(&cfg); err != nil {
111: 		return nil, err
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	return &cfg, nil
115: }
116: 
117: // List returns all valid agent configurations from the agents directory.
118: // Invalid files are silently skipped. If the agents directory does not exist,
119: // an empty slice is returned.
120: func List() ([]AgentConfig, error) {
121: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
122: 	if err != nil {
123: 		return nil, err
124: 	}
125: 
126: 	agentsDir := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents")
127: 
128: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(agentsDir)
129: 	if err != nil {
130: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
131: 			return []AgentConfig{}, nil
132: 		}
133: 		return nil, err
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	var agents []AgentConfig
137: 	for _, entry := range entries {
138: 		if entry.IsDir() {
139: 			continue
140: 		}
141: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml") {
142: 			continue
143: 		}
144: 		name := strings.TrimSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml")
145: 		cfg, err := Load(name)
146: 		if err != nil {
147: 			continue // skip invalid files
148: 		}
149: 		agents = append(agents, *cfg)
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	return agents, nil
153: }
154: 
155: // Scaffold returns a TOML template string for a new agent configuration.
156: // The name argument is interpolated into the template.
157: func Scaffold(name string) (string, error) {
158: 	tmpl := `name = "` + name + `"
159: description = ""
160: 
161: # Full provider/model per models.dev
162: model = "provider/model-name"
163: 
164: # Agent persona (optional)
165: # system_prompt = ""
166: 
167: # Default skill (optional, can be overridden with --skill flag)
168: # skill = ""
169: 
170: # Context files - glob patterns resolved from workdir or cwd (optional)
171: # files = []
172: 
173: # Working directory (optional)
174: # workdir = ""
175: 
176: # Tools this agent can use (optional)
177: # Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch
178: # tools = []
179: 
180: # Sub-agents this agent can invoke (optional)
181: # sub_agents = []
182: 
183: # [sub_agents_config]
184: # max_depth = 3
185: # parallel = true
186: # timeout = 120
187: 
188: # [memory]
189: # enabled = false
190: # path = ""
191: # last_n = 10
192: # max_entries = 100
193: 
194: # [params]
195: # temperature = 0.3
196: # max_tokens = 4096
197: `
198: 	return tmpl, nil
199: }
200: 
201: // tomlDecode is a package-level wrapper for toml.Decode, used by tests.
202: var tomlDecode = toml.Decode

(End of file - total 202 lines)
</content>

package agent

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/BurntSushi/toml"
	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
)

// MemoryConfig holds memory sub-configuration for an agent.
type MemoryConfig struct {
	Enabled    bool   `toml:"enabled"`
	Path       string `toml:"path"`
	LastN      int    `toml:"last_n"`
	MaxEntries int    `toml:"max_entries"`

parts.text:
Here are the exact hotspots to update.

## 1) Tool registration point

**File:** `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry.go`

**Relevant lines:** `88-99` (especially `90`, `92-99`)

```go
88: // RegisterAll registers all implemented tool entries into the given registry.
89: // Both cmd/run.go and ExecuteCallAgent call this after NewRegistry().
90: // Future milestones add lines here — no call-site changes needed.
91: // Safe to call multiple times (Register silently replaces duplicates).
92: func RegisterAll(r *Registry) {
93: 	r.Register(toolname.ListDirectory, listDirectoryEntry())
94: 	r.Register(toolname.ReadFile, readFileEntry())
95: 	r.Register(toolname.WriteFile, writeFileEntry())
96: 	r.Register(toolname.EditFile, editFileEntry())
97: 	r.Register(toolname.RunCommand, runCommandEntry())
98: 	r.Register(toolname.URLFetch, urlFetchEntry())
99: }
```

**What changes here:** add one more `r.Register(toolname.<NewTool>, <newTool>Entry())` line in `RegisterAll`.

---

## 2) Registry tests to mirror for new tool

**File:** `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/registry_test.go`

### a) Pattern to copy for “registered” test

**Relevant lines:** `409-416`

```go
409: func TestRegisterAll_RegistersURLFetch(t *testing.T) {
410: 	r := NewRegistry()
411: 	RegisterAll(r)
412:
413: 	if !r.Has(toolname.URLFetch) {
414: 		t.Fatalf("Has(%q) returned false after RegisterAll", toolname.URLFetch)
415: 	}
416: }
```

### b) Pattern to copy for “resolves” test

**Relevant lines:** `418-435`

```go
418: func TestRegisterAll_ResolvesURLFetch(t *testing.T) {
419: 	r := NewRegistry()
420: 	RegisterAll(r)
421:
422: 	tools, err := r.Resolve([]string{toolname.URLFetch})
423: 	if err != nil {
424: 		t.Fatalf("Resolve returned error: %v", err)
425: 	}
426: 	if len(tools) != 1 {
427: 		t.Fatalf("expected 1 tool, got %d", len(tools))
428: 	}
429: 	if tools[0].Name != toolname.URLFetch {
430: 		t.Errorf("tool Name: got %q, want %q", tools[0].Name, toolname.URLFetch)
431: 	}
432: 	if _, ok := tools[0].Parameters["url"]; !ok {
433: 		t.Error("expected tool to have a 'url' parameter")
434: 	}
435: }
```

### c) Idempotency test already covers all registrations

**Relevant lines:** `437-445`

```go
437: func TestRegisterAll_Idempotent(t *testing.T) {
438: 	r := NewRegistry()
439: 	RegisterAll(r)
440: 	RegisterAll(r) // second call should not panic
...
445: }
```

**What changes here:** add two new tests analogous to URLFetch (`Registers<NewTool>`, `Resolves<NewTool>`), and assert expected parameters for the new tool definition.

---

## 3) Agent scaffold comment + validation path

**File:** `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent/agent.go`

### a) Validation path (indirectly picks up tool list from `toolname.ValidNames()`)

**Relevant lines:** `77-82`

```go
77: 	validTools := toolname.ValidNames()
78: 	for _, name := range cfg.Tools {
79: 		if !validTools[name] {
80: 			return fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q in tools config", name)
81: 		}
82: 	}
```

**Implication:** no hardcoded tool list here; once `toolname.ValidNames()` includes the new tool, validation accepts it.

### b) Scaffold comment hardcoded list (must update)

**Relevant lines:** `176-178`

```go
176: # Tools this agent can use (optional)
177: # Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch
178: # tools = []
```

**What changes here:** extend line 177 to include the new tool name.

---

Quick extra note: there is also a scaffold assertion in `/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/agent/agent_test.go` (line `924`) that checks this exact comment string, so that test will need updating too when you change `agent.go` scaffold text.
